verdict_reasoning: TMED10 is a type I membrane protein (single-pass TM with GOLD domain) canonically localized to the Golgi and ER with a documented PM pool. The NCBI summary explicitly states 'localized to the plasma membrane and golgi cisternae,' indicating dual localization. As a member of the EMP24/GP25L/p24 trafficking-protein family — a COPII/COPII-cargo protein family involved in ER-Golgi vesicular trafficking — it cycles between the ER and Golgi via vesicular carriers and has a documented minority surface pool from secretory cycling. The PM residency is real but not the dominant compartment; it qualifies as contextual dual_localization rather than canonical surface receptor.
predicted_key_uncertainty: Magnitude of PM pool and stability of PM residence under baseline conditions not quantified; p24-family proteins are poorly studied for cell-surface accessibility relative to their Golgi/ER trafficking function. Unclear whether PM residency supports binder access or is primarily transient cycling.
